stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers, llama, and text-generation.
This indicates ongoing community interest and practical usage of Meta's Llama model variants.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
This indicates continued community interest and benchmarking activity around Meta's Llama family of models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline indicates the model is being used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance gains.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads on Apple hardware.
This feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging applications with local AI coding agents.
The key feature emphasizes running all AI processes entirely on the user's own device for privacy/control.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification.
The models are available in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are permissively licensed under Apache 2.0.
The partnership involves ROOST (Robust Open Online Safety Tools) to enhance safety capabilities.

stories.title: Hackers are learning to exploit chatbot ‘personalities’
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: Hackers are developing new methods to exploit the 'personalities' embedded within AI chatbots.
The vulnerability represents an evolution from the simple exploits seen in earlier generations of AI.
This highlights growing security risks associated with the increasingly complex and personalized nature of modern AI models.

stories.title: Agentic Proving for Program Verification
stories.author: Bas Spitters
bullet_points: The research evaluates the capability of Claude Code in an agentic proving framework for program verification.
The system achieved a high rate of generating arguably valid specifications (98.8%) on the CLEVER benchmark.
This work connects advanced agentic AI systems to the critical field of formal program verification.

stories.title: MemAudit: Post-hoc Auditing of Poisoned Agent Memory via Causal Attribution and Structural Anomaly Detection
stories.author: Lin Sun
bullet_points: Addresses a critical security vulnerability in LLM agents: memory poisoning via adversarial user input.
Proposes 'MemAudit,' a post-hoc auditing technique using Causal Attribution and Structural Anomaly Detection.
Moves beyond existing online defenses by focusing on detecting malicious records injected into persistent agent memory.

stories.title: CVSearch: Empowering Multimodal LLMs with Cognitive Visual Search for High-Resolution Image Perception
stories.author: Yaowei Wang
bullet_points: CVSearch addresses a critical bottleneck in Multimodal LLMs (MLLMs): high-resolution (HR) image perception.
It resolves the trade-off between visual search coverage and computational efficiency, which plagues existing methods.
The proposed method improves upon existing visual expert-assisted and scan-based search techniques.

stories.title: One Policy, Infinite NPCs: Persona-Traceable Shared RL Policies for Scalable Game Agents
stories.author: Yoosung Hong
bullet_points: Introduces 'pcsp,' a new method for creating scalable game agents with distinct, traceable personalities (NPCs).
Achieves high performance in persona identification (17x above chance) and strong semantic-behavioral alignment (Spearman rho approx 0.73) on a 300-persona benchmark.
Demonstrates significant efficiency gains, achieving 22x faster inference compared to LLM-as-policy baselines for life simulation games.

stories.title: The physics of AI weather models
stories.author: Kirsten I. Tempest
bullet_points: The research investigates whether AI weather models implicitly solve underlying physical equations governing the atmosphere.
The study uses Centered Kernel Alignment (CKA) to show that diverse AI weather models represent atmospheric physics in similar ways.
It suggests that the architecture and training methods of AI models constrain the form of the physical laws they can simulate.

stories.title: LLM-driven design of physics-constrained constitutive models: two agents are better than one
stories.author: Christian Cyron
bullet_points: The research addresses the complexity of creating physics-constrained constitutive models, traditionally requiring deep expertise across multiple fields.
It introduces a novel multi-agent LLM framework to systematically generate and validate these models, overcoming the limitations of single-agent pipelines.
This work represents a significant step in applying advanced AI (LLMs) to complex scientific domains like continuum mechanics.

stories.title: OpenSkillEval: Automatically Auditing the Open Skill Ecosystem for LLM Agents
stories.author: Yixin Cao
bullet_points: Introduces OpenSkillEval, a framework designed to automatically audit the open skill ecosystem for LLM agents.
Addresses critical gaps in the field, specifically how different models interact with skills and how to evaluate skill quality.
Focuses on practical concerns for users, such as making informed selections based on cost-performance trade-offs.

stories.title: DeepSeek reasonix, DeepSeek native coding agent with high caching and low cost
stories.author: Alifatisk
bullet_points: DeepSeek has released 'reasonix,' a native coding agent designed for high efficiency.
The agent features high caching capabilities and is noted for its low operational cost.
The news is relevant to the competitive landscape of AI coding assistants and LLM deployment.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational AI tasks.
It is associated with the deepseek_v3 architecture and utilizes standard ML formats like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It integrates various coding models such as Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental local image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for macOS users.
Support for Windows and Linux is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has expanded its capabilities by integrating compatibility with the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage advanced models, such as Claude Code, within the Ollama ecosystem.
The feature supports running powerful, proprietary models alongside open-source alternatives via Ollama.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code in a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Why Nuro thinks being a robotaxi ‘second mover’ gives it an advantage
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: Waymo is established as the dominant leader in the robotaxi market with a large operational fleet.
The article explores the strategic advantage Nuro might gain by being a 'second mover' in the industry.
Competition in the autonomous vehicle space is fierce, involving Tesla, Zoox, Avride, and Motional.

stories.title: SpaceX's Starship V3—still a work in progress—mostly successful on first flight
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: SpaceX's Starship V3 flight was mostly successful, but remains a work in progress.
The vehicle has not yet achieved the goal of flying to low-Earth orbit.
The report suggests SpaceX still has significant development hurdles to overcome.

stories.title: Four Russian satellites are now within striking distance of an ICEYE radarsat
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: Russian satellites are reportedly close enough to strike an ICEYE radarsat.
The capability described is noted as unusual for satellites performing standard missions.
The story touches upon geopolitical tensions involving advanced space technology.

stories.title: US scrambles to stop Internet users re-creating dead pilots’ voices
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: The story concerns the misuse of AI technology to recreate the voices of deceased pilots.
This activity is complicated by existing regulations that restrict the public release of cockpit audio recordings by the NTSB.
The current workaround employed by users appears to bypass these established legal disclosures.

stories.title: Beyond Binary Edits Robust Multimodal Knowledge Editing with Adversarial Subspace Alignment
stories.author: Chaochao Chen
bullet_points: The research addresses a key challenge in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs): efficiently updating knowledge without performance degradation.
Existing knowledge editing methods lack generality, failing to apply edits consistently across semantically equivalent visual and linguistic variations.
The proposed solution uses Adversarial Subspace Alignment to improve edit propagation and overcome limitations caused by rigid scopes and sample bias.

stories.title: PhotoFlow: Agentic 3D Virtual Photography Missions
stories.author: Zhihang Zhong
bullet_points: Introduces 'PhotoFlow,' an agentic system for 3D virtual photography.
The system allows an AI agent to autonomously select a camera pose and render a photograph based only on a language intent and a 3D scene.
The research highlights the difficulty in evaluating the combined capabilities of complex 3D spatial understanding and abstract aesthetic judgment in these agents.

stories.title: Any2Any: Efficient Cross-Embodiment Transfer for Humanoid Whole-Body Tracking
stories.author: Hua Chen
bullet_points: Introduces 'Any2Any,' a method for efficient cross-embodiment transfer of Whole-Body Tracking (WBT) models.
Addresses the high computational cost and data requirements of training WBT models from scratch for humanoid robots.
Aims to allow pretrained WBT specialists to be adapted with minimal effort across different humanoid platforms.

stories.title: OnePred: Next-Query Prediction via Recursive Intent Memory in Multi-Turn Conversations
stories.author: Guanjun Jiang
bullet_points: The research addresses the reactive nature of current LLM conversational systems by focusing on next-query prediction.
The proposed method, OnePred, utilizes 'Recursive Intent Memory' to anticipate user queries based on prior dialogue.
The work aims to overcome existing limitations, such as the lack of dedicated benchmarks and the efficiency-quality trade-off in dialogue modeling.

stories.title: Learning Through Noise: Why Subliminal Learning Works and When It Fails
stories.author: Viola Priesemann
bullet_points: The paper investigates 'subliminal learning' in neural networks, defined as knowledge transfer via distillation on unrelated input-output pairs.
Previous theories linked this effect to similar teacher-student initializations, but the research challenges this necessity.
The study proposes that compatible output heads, rather than initialization matching, govern the phenomenon of subliminal learning.

stories.title: DualMem: Bypassing the Objectness Bottleneck for Calibrated Unknown-Stream Filtering in Open-World Object Detection
stories.author: Siyuan Chen
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of Open-World Object Detection (OWOD), which requires identifying both known and novel (unknown) objects.
The study identifies an 'information bottleneck' in current OWOD detectors, where unknown prediction streams are heavily contaminated by background false positives (46-71%).
The proposed method, DualMem, aims to bypass this bottleneck to improve the calibration and filtering of unknown object predictions for future learning.

stories.title: Advanced AI Service Provisioning in O-RAN through LLM Engine Integration
stories.author: Vijay K. Shah
bullet_points: The paper addresses the manual and slow process of developing and deploying AI applications within the O-RAN architecture.
It proposes a 'Dual-Brain' architecture to leverage LLM reasoning capabilities while meeting the real-time, deterministic inference needs of RAN control.
The research focuses on integrating advanced AI service provisioning into O-RAN using LLMs, a key area for 6G and future wireless networks.

stories.title: Debiased Negative Mining Improves Out-of-distribution Detection with Pre-trained Vision-Language Models
stories.author: Zhen Fang
bullet_points: The research addresses Out-of-Distribution (OOD) detection, a critical area for improving ML model reliability.
It focuses on enhancing post-hoc OOD detection specifically when using pre-trained Vision-Language Models (VLMs).
The proposed method involves 'Debiased Negative Mining' to improve the accuracy of detecting unexpected inputs.

stories.title: SeedER: Seed-and-Expand Retrieval from Knowledge Graphs
stories.author: Emmanuel Noutahi
bullet_points: Introduces SeedER, a new retrieval framework designed to overcome challenges in Knowledge Graph (KG) retrieval.
Addresses limitations of existing methods, specifically the rapid growth of ego-graph expansion and difficulties with multi-hop queries in dense embeddings.
SeedER leverages KG structure using iterative, low-cost expansion techniques to enable scalable retrieval.

stories.title: Approaching I/O-optimality for Approximate Attention
stories.author: Anastasios Zouzias
bullet_points: The research focuses on optimizing the I/O complexity of the attention mechanism in Large Language Models (LLMs).
It addresses the memory bottleneck by minimizing data transfers between fast and slow memory when computing the attention matrix.
The work aims to improve upon existing methods like FlashAttention, which currently have an I/O cost quadratic in the sequence length (n).

stories.title: Contrast to Detect: Dynamic Graph Contrastive Regularization for Unsupervised Anomaly Detection in Multivariate Time Series
stories.author: John Cartlidge
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of unsupervised anomaly detection in Multivariate Time Series (MTS) where labels are unavailable.
Identifies limitations in current methods: reconstruction-based detectors fail to distinguish anomalies, and existing graph contrastive methods assume a static relational structure.
Proposes 'Dynamic Graph Contrastive Regularization' to handle dynamic inter-variable dependencies and structural drift in MTS data.

stories.title: ChartFI: Benchmarking Faithfulness and Insightfulness of Chart Descriptions from Multimodal Large Language Models
stories.author: Siming Chen
bullet_points: The research addresses the critical need to evaluate how accurately and insightfully Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) describe charts.
Current benchmarks for chart description generation are deemed insufficient because they use simple, homogeneous charts and shallow descriptions.
Faithful and insightful chart descriptions are crucial for accessibility, cross-modal retrieval, and data extraction from complex visualizations.

stories.title: OnePred: Next-Query Prediction via Recursive Intent Memory in Multi-Turn Conversations
stories.author: Guanjun Jiang
bullet_points: The research addresses the reactive nature of current LLM conversational systems by focusing on next-query prediction.
The proposed method, OnePred, utilizes Recursive Intent Memory to anticipate user queries based on prior dialogue.
The work aims to overcome existing challenges, specifically the lack of dedicated benchmarks and the efficiency-quality trade-off in this task.

stories.title: Benchmarking Google Embeddings 2 against Open-Source Models for Multilingual Dense Retrieval and RAG Systems
stories.author: Giandomenico Solimando
bullet_points: The study benchmarks Google Embeddings 2 (GE2) against five leading open-source multilingual models (BGE-M3, E5-large, etc.).
GE2 demonstrated superior performance across four BEIR subsets and a synthetic Italian RAG corpus.
The research also evaluates practical aspects like chunking strategies and per-query latency on CPU hardware.

stories.title: DiLaDiff: Distilled Latent-Augmented Diffusion for Language Modeling
stories.author: Ante Jukić
bullet_points: Proposes DiLaDiff, a new masked diffusion language model variant designed to improve language modeling.
Addresses the inherent issue in diffusion models of failing to capture correlations between decoded tokens, balancing quality and throughput.
Utilizes a continuous latent space learned via an auto-encoder fine-tuned from an existing masked diffusion model.

stories.title: Structure-Guided Entity Resolution: Fine-Tuning LLMs for Robust Name Matching in Complex Linguistic Contexts
stories.author: Nilesh Patil
bullet_points: The research addresses the complex problem of entity resolution, specifically matching person names across diverse datasets.
It focuses on using fine-tuning of Large Language Models (LLMs) to improve name matching robustness in complex linguistic contexts.
The application is highly relevant to critical industry needs, such as Know Your Customer (KYC) compliance, where data inconsistency is a major hurdle.

stories.title: Memory has grown to nearly two-thirds of AI chip component costs
stories.author: intelkishan
bullet_points: Memory is becoming a dominant cost driver in AI chip manufacturing.
The cost contribution of memory now approaches two-thirds of the total AI chip component expense.
This highlights a critical hardware bottleneck and cost factor in the scaling of AI infrastructure.

stories.title: I Ditched Cloud LLMs for Gemma 4 4B: A DevOps Engineer's 48-Hour Reality Check
stories.author: asamaes
bullet_points: A DevOps engineer compared using cloud-based LLMs against the open-weights Gemma 4 4B model.
The article details a 48-hour practical evaluation of running Gemma 4 in a real-world DevOps context.
The piece serves as a submission for a specific challenge related to Gemma 4, focusing on deployment realities.

stories.title: Audit AI-Generated PRs Before You Merge Them (Swarm Orchestrator 10.3.0)
stories.author: moonrunnerkc
bullet_points: Introduces a CLI and GitHub Action to audit AI-generated Pull Requests (PRs).
The tool scans PRs for 'sketchy patterns' and provides advisory feedback directly within the PR.
This addresses the growing need for quality control and verification in AI-assisted software development workflows.
